key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share

key_assumptions.source: FCFA2S reinvested at hurdle; Q1 2026 +20% revenue / 6% organic supports pipeline

open_questions: Confirm CSU.TO price and USD conversion for live IRR.
Extract Q1 2026 MDA / financial statements (inventory tier, 0 chars in evidence build).
Map Topicus/Lumine/Volaris stake NAV vs consolidated.
thesis: Constellation Software Inc. is a Canadian holding company that acquires and holds vertical market software (VMS) businesses. **[Fact]** Customers are niche industry users paying maintenance and license fees on mission-critical software. **[Fact]** Cash is generated as free cash flow available to shareholders (FCFA2S) and redeployed almost entirely into new acquisitions at hurdle rates. **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue was **$11.62B** (+15%); Q1 2026 revenue **$3.18B** (+20%) with organic growth disclosed in the shareholder letter. **[Fact]** FY2025 net income was **$586M** (-24% YoY) while Q4 2025 net income was **$166M** (-48% vs prior Q4). **[Inference]** GAAP net income is a poor owner metric because earnout fair-value swings and Lumine investment revaluations distort reported earnings.
why_market_wrong: There is **no clear dated mispricing signal** (HK predictive attribute: **none**). **[Inference]** Generalists focus on GAAP net income volatility and law-of-large-numbers concerns, while the owner metric FCFA2S (**$79.42/sh** FY2025) compounds steadily. Return is serial acquirer reinvestment at price: the question is whether hurdle-rate deals remain available as scale grows, not whether a hidden asset is marked wrong tomorrow.
